name: From Puente Genil to Cerro Becerrero – circular via Estepa
name_original: Desde Puente Genil a Cerro Becerrero – circular por Estepa
region_intro: Road cycling around Puente Genil offers routes through a landscape dominated by extensive olive groves and vineyards, characteristic of Andalusia. The region features well-developed greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite, which follow former railway lines with gentle gradients. These paths provide traffic-free cycling experiences, traversing agricultural lands and crossing historical viaducts. The terrain generally presents minimal inclines, making it accessible for various cycling abilities, with some routes offering more challenging elevation gains. Best road cycling routes around Puente Genil The most popular road cycling route is Casariche and Badolatosa — circular from, a 32.4 miles (52.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a scenic circular journey through the local countryside. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is From Puente Genil to Cerro Becerrero – circular via Estepa, a difficult 33.7 miles (54.2 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, providing a challenging ride with expansive views. Local road cyclists also love the Castillo del Moral – Church of San Mateo, Lucena loop from Puente Genil, a 44.1 miles (71.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 14 minutes. Road cycling around Puente Genil is defined by endless olive groves, vibrant vineyards, and traffic-free greenways. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy rides to more challenging routes with notable climbs. The routes in Puente Genil are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 5 reviews. More than 700 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Puente Genil's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Puente Genil?
A: There are over 60 road cycling routes available around Puente Genil, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. You'll find everything from easy rides to more challenging routes with significant climbs.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Puente Genil?
A: The terrain around Puente Genil is predominantly characterized by endless olive groves and vibrant vineyards. Many routes follow well-developed greenways, like the Vía Verde del Aceite, which are former railway lines with gentle gradients, making them largely traffic-free and accessible. While many routes have minimal inclines, some do offer more challenging elevation gains for experienced cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Puente Genil?
A: Yes, Puente Genil offers several easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners. The greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite and the Greenway of the Subbética, are known for their gentle slopes (maximum gradient of 2-3%) and absence of motor vehicles, providing a safe and pleasant introduction to road cycling in the region.

Q: Can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs near Puente Genil?
A: Absolutely. While many routes are gentle, there are challenging options for those seeking significant elevation gain. For example, the route &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994381575/from-puente-genil-to-cerro-becerrero-circular-via-estepa" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Puente Genil to Cerro Becerrero – circular via Estepa&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 54.2 km path that includes notable climbs and expansive views, providing a demanding ride.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see along the cycling routes?
A: Cycling routes around Puente Genil are rich with natural beauty and historical landmarks. You can explore the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5392067" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Genil River Meander&lt;/a&gt;, visit the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Zóñar Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; (a wetland of international importance), or pass through vast &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/893911" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla Vineyards&lt;/a&gt;. Many routes also feature restored railway stations, which now serve as charming rest stops.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Puente Genil are circular, allowing you to start and end your ride in the same location. A popular example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1005765382/casariche-and-badolatosa-circular-from" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Casariche and Badolatosa — circular from&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 52.2 km journey through the local countryside.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Puente Genil?
A: The best time for road cycling in Puente Genil is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November). During these seasons, the weather is mild and pleasant, making for comfortable riding conditions. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can be cooler and wetter.

Q: Are the cycling routes in Puente Genil suitable for families?
A: Yes, the greenways in Puente Genil, particularly the Vía Verde del Aceite and the Greenway of the Subbética, are ideal for family cycling. Their gentle gradients, traffic-free environment, and scenic beauty make them safe and enjoyable for riders of all ages and abilities. Many restored railway stations along these routes offer amenities like cafes and visitor centers.

Q: Where can I find parking for road cycling routes in Puente Genil?
A: Parking is generally available in Puente Genil itself, especially near access points to the Vía Verde del Aceite and other greenways. Many of the restored railway stations along these routes also offer designated parking areas, making it convenient to start your cycling adventure.

Q: How do other cyclists rate the road cycling experience in Puente Genil?
A: The road cycling experience in Puente Genil is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 7 reviews. Cyclists often praise the region's beautiful olive groves, traffic-free greenways, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels.

Q: Are there options for long-distance road cycling from Puente Genil?
A: Yes, Puente Genil serves as an excellent base for long-distance road cycling. The Vía Verde del Aceite alone stretches for approximately 120-128 kilometers, offering extensive opportunities for longer rides. Routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128239" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of Fernán Núñez – Plaza de San José loop from Puente Genil&lt;/a&gt; cover over 90 km, providing a substantial challenge.

Q: Can I access the greenways using public transport?
A: Access to the greenways, such as the Vía Verde del Aceite and the Greenway of the Subbética, can often be facilitated by public transport to nearby towns or directly to Puente Genil. Some restored railway stations along the greenways may also be accessible via local bus services, though it's advisable to check specific routes and schedules in advance.

Q: Are there places to eat or rest along the cycling routes?
A: Yes, many of the restored railway stations along the Vía Verde routes have been converted into welcoming spots for cyclists. These often house restaurants, cafes, and visitor centers, providing excellent opportunities for a break, a meal, or to refill water bottles. Additionally, cycling through picturesque villages like Zuheros or Doña Mencía offers chances to find local eateries.
tips: The name of this pretty town comes from the river Genil, which runs through this town. Puente Genil has several examples of civil architecture, among them: Stewardship of the Dukes of Medinaceli, Teatro Circo, Casa Consistorial.
The hill of Estepa repeaters, wide road in good condition, little traffic. Nice views of Estepa (for example, Cerro de San Cristóbal and Torre de la Victoria).

The construction of the tower of the church of La Victoria finished in 1776. Declared an Asset of Cultural Interest Historical Heritage of Spain and is the only thing that remains of the abandoned and demolished church in 1939.
